What We Do

Our Impact Programs

For over 86 years, the Kiwanis Club of Stuart has delivered programs that serve children and families throughout Martin County. Every initiative is driven by the dedication of our members and the generosity of our community.

Literacy

Kiwanis Reads

Kiwanis Reads is our flagship literacy initiative, dedicated to putting books in the hands of every child in Martin County. We believe that reading is the foundation for lifelong success, and our programs make that foundation accessible to all.

Through Little Free Libraries installed throughout Kiwanis Park, children and families can take and share books year-round. Our Celebrity Readers in the Park events bring community leaders, educators, and local figures to read aloud to children in an engaging outdoor setting.

We also distribute free books at the Martin County Fair Reading Booth, at Pancakes & Reading events, and during our monthly Super Saturday gatherings at the park.

Support Kiwanis Reads Follow Us on Facebook
Community Giving

Saturday with Santa

For more than 60 years, our Saturday with Santa program has clothed underprivileged children in Martin County. Each year, we partner with local schools and social services to identify children in need and provide them with a complete outfit and new shoes.

A donation of just $75 provides one child with a full set of clothing -- head to toe. It's one of our longest-running traditions and one of the most direct ways our members impact families in our community.

Volunteers help with shopping, wrapping, and distribution. If you'd like to sponsor a child or volunteer your time, we'd love to have you involved.

Clothe a Child -- $75 Volunteer With Us
Monthly Events

Super Saturday

Super Saturday is a monthly community event held at Kiwanis Park at Woodlawn. Families come together for games, reading activities, and free programming in a safe and welcoming outdoor environment.

Each Super Saturday features activities for children of all ages, volunteer readers, and community resource tables. It's a true reflection of what Kiwanis is about: neighbors serving neighbors.

See Upcoming Dates Volunteer
Signature Event

Pancakes & Reading in the Park

One of our most beloved annual events, Pancakes & Reading in the Park brings the community together for a morning of food, fun, and literacy. Families enjoy a free pancake breakfast at Kiwanis Park while children participate in reading activities led by community volunteers.

The event is free and open to the public. It celebrates our commitment to early literacy while building community connections in a festive, family-friendly atmosphere.

View Event Calendar Get Involved
Scholarships

Robert Whitty Agricultural Scholarship

Named in honor of longtime member Robert Whitty, this scholarship supports Martin County students pursuing careers in agriculture. The award recognizes academic achievement, community involvement, and dedication to the agricultural field.

Agriculture is a cornerstone of Martin County's heritage and economy. This scholarship ensures the next generation of agricultural leaders has the support they need to succeed.

For information on eligibility requirements and how to apply, please contact us directly.

Contact Us to Apply

Robert Whitty
Agricultural Scholarship

Martin County
Fair

Annual Fundraiser

Martin County Fair

Each year, the Kiwanis Club of Stuart operates two booths at the Martin County Fair: our Beer Booth, which is one of our primary annual fundraisers, and our Reading Booth, where we distribute free books to children and families.

Fair week is one of the most active times of year for our club, bringing together members, volunteers, and the broader community. Proceeds from the Beer Booth directly fund our programs for children throughout the year.

Volunteers are always needed during Fair week. If you'd like to help staff a booth or get involved, reach out to us.

Volunteer at the Fair View Fair Dates
